2605.19518.title: BLINKG: A Benchmark for LLM-Integrated Knowledge Graph Generation
2605.19518.paper_summary: Generating Knowledge Graphs (KGs) remains one of the most time-consuming and labor-intensive tasks for knowledge engineers, as they need to identify semantic equivalences between input data sources and ontology terms. While declarative solutions (e.g., RML, SPARQL-Anything) have helped to generalize this process, aligning input schema elements with ontology terms still involves intricate transformations and requires considerable manual effort. With the advent of Large Language Models (LLMs), there is growing interest in leveraging their capabilities to assist KG engineers. Although some studies have explored using LLMs to automate KG construction, there is still no standardized framework for assessing how effectively they establish correspondences between data schemes and ontology concepts. Therefore, in this paper, we propose BLINKG, a benchmark designed to evaluate the mapping capabilities of LLMs in constructing KGs from heterogeneous data sources. The benchmark includes a set of scenarios with increasing complexity, based on real-world use cases. We conduct an extensive experimental evaluation of several stateof-the-art LLMs using BLINK and observe that they already offer promising solutions. However, their performance remains limited in complex scenarios. Thanks to this benchmark, we can already assess the current capabilities of LLMs for KG construction. Additionally, we define a set of requirements for achieving (semi)automated (LLM-driven) KG construction, opening new research lines in this area.
2605.19518.paper_summary_zh: 生成知識圖譜（KGs）仍然是知識工程師最耗時且勞動密集的任務之一，因為他們需要識別輸入數據源與本體術語之間的語義等價關係。雖然聲明式解決方案（例如 RML、SPARQL-Anything）已經幫助將這個過程進行一般化，但將輸入模式元素與本體術語對齊仍然涉及複雜的轉換，並且需要相當大的手動努力。隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）的出現，越來越多的人對利用它們的能力來協助 KG 工程師產生興趣。儘管一些研究已經探討了使用 LLMs 自動化 KG 構建，但仍然沒有標準化的框架來評估它們在數據方案與本體概念之間建立對應的有效性。因此，在本文中，我們提出了 BLINKG，一個旨在評估 LLMs 在從異構數據源構建 KGs 方面的映射能力的基準。該基準包括一組基於現實世界用例的逐步增加複雜性的場景。我們使用 BLINK 對幾個最先進的 LLMs 進行了廣泛的實驗評估，並觀察到它們已經提供了有前景的解決方案。然而，它們在複雜場景中的表現仍然有限。得益於這個基準，我們已經可以評估 LLMs 在 KG 構建方面的當前能力。此外，我們定義了一組要求，以實現（半）自動化的（LLM 驅動的）KG 構建，為該領域開啟了新的研究方向。
2605.19518.author: Carla Castedo et.al.
2605.19518.authors: Carla Castedo, Enrique Iglesias, Manuel Lama, Alberto Bugarin-Diz, Maria-Esther Vidal, David Chaves-Fraga
